title: Deploy municipal bonds to give urban India the makeover it needs—Here’s how to attract investors
description: Urban local bodies (ULBs) must mobilize vast sums to modernize India’s rapidly expanding cities and keep them liveable. Municipal bonds are a useful way to raise that capital. But India must first strengthen the market for ULB debt. Here’s what could be done.

title: Mint Quick Edit | End of Iran’s regime? Why prediction markets generate both information and anxiety
description: Bets on crystal-ball platforms like Polymarket over war outcomes and the like reveal what people think. That makes them useful. But what about insider trading? And perverse incentives to make ‘killings’ in such prediction markets?

title: A 20-year trip from 7-day failure to long-term rule
description: The question remains; will Nishant be able to fill in the boots left by his father? The other question is who would control the government in Bihar?

title: Event prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket are buzzing in America: Should India allow them too?
description: Prediction markets on platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi are witnessing a boom, with wagers being made on outcomes of war, elections, sports and more. Their appeal is undeniable—even as questions swirl over regulation and insider information.

title: Battle of pipelines: Hormuz is clamped but other routes exist for Gulf oil to flow—if Iran lets it
description: Global oil markets may stabilize if even a modest share of Gulf supply can avoid the clamped Strait of Hormuz. Saudi Arabia and the UAE are activating bypass pipelines to reroute exports. But Iran’s gameplan is to worsen today’s oil shock.

title: What explains upmarket Apple’s low-priced MacBook? A chip crunch more than market opportunity
description: Apple has long guarded its premium aura, but slowing laptop sales and a global chip shortage may have forced a rethink. Its low-priced MacBook aimed at students aims to mop up sales in a high-volume segment even as a supply chain squeeze threatens high-end sales this year.

title: Gold-dollar dynamics: What the US ‘ashvamedha’ run and AI could mean global for investors
description: Investors betting on gold’s rise and the dollar’s decline as a reserve currency may need to rethink their assumptions. The Iran war has seen the dollar strengthen and gold fall. Will this last? Here are four scenarios for investors around the world to track.

title: Ajit Ranade: India’s stubbornly high PF payout rate is getting in the way of its monetary policy
description: The EPFO’s 8.25% payout is 3 percentage points above the Reserve Bank of India’s policy rate. For RBI’s credit easing to take effect, the PF rate must be linked in some way with rates in the larger savings market. When will India’s political economy let this distortion end?

title: Marketers, take note: The spending patterns of Indian households change in distinct ways as incomes go up
description: Nobody can bank on assumptions of linear growth in spending by Indian households as they earn more. As a survey reveals, different income brackets display distinct patterns—with new market categories entering the picture—as key thresholds are crossed.

title: Prudent path: India should stay resolutely neutral as wars erupt in a world without rules
description: India has stakes across the world’s geopolitical ruptures—as evident in Europe, West Asia and potentially even East Asia. In such a dangerously fractured world, the wisest course is strategic aloofness: focus on managing the fallout of wars while keeping a safe distance.

title: The odds of financial crises are rising. Will the US be there to help?
description: The U.S. historically leads recovery efforts. That is becoming less certain, Robert Hormats writes in a guest commentary.

title: Uncertainty amid the Iran War: Why the answers to India’s external troubles lie squarely at home
description: As war in West Asia drives up oil prices and geopolitical uncertainty, India’s exposure to external risks has grown. In response, we need a renewed focus on sound economic policy, private sector investment and job generation.

title: Clive Crook: Just how resilient is the US economy? The Iran war could turn out to be a test
description: Inflation risk, tariff turmoil and a brazen fiscal overstretch were already testing the US economy. The fallout of the Iran war now adds uncertainty that could push an indebted America to an economic reckoning should hostilities refuse to follow its script.

title: US-Israeli attack on Iran: Under what conditions can an illegal war claim moral legitimacy?
description: Armed aggression that violates international law can sometimes claim moral legitimacy. History offers rare examples. But does the US-Israeli attack on Iran meet that test—or does it instead reveal how far today’s leaders are willing to discard both law and legitimacy?

title: Manu Joseph: Artificial intelligence isn’t funny and seems unable to grasp why that’s so
description: AI can write essays, poems and code, but ask it to be funny and it fails. Ask it why it isn’t funny and it fails again. What it doesn’t get is that humour is a form of human excellence—and that’s rare even among humans.

title: Lala Shri Ram: How a failed entrepreneur built one of India's industrial dynasties
description: For a man who arrived in his family business without any credentials, Lala Shri Ram left behind two of India’s best colleges, a research institute, and one of independent India's largest industrial houses.

title: Have you noticed your AI assistant’s favourite words?
description: Chatbots tend to overuse some words until they’ve become the fingerprints that identify AI-produced content.

title: A guilt gap in Indian kitchens is a $50 bn market for clean-label ingredients. E.g.: idli, dosa batter
description: The Indian kitchen faces a conflict between modern convenience and traditional values, leading to an 85% adoption gap in the ready-to-eat market. Successful brands will enable cooking while respecting tradition, creating a culinary experience for consumers who seek engagement over replacement.

title: Operation Epic Fury sparks a high-stakes balancing act in India
description: As the West Asian conflict intensifies, New Delhi navigates a precarious diplomatic path to safeguard vital energy supplies and millions of overseas citizens.

title: Jack Dorsey shouldn’t scare people: Every employer needn’t deploy AI to lay human workers off
description: Fears of artificial intelligence gobbling up jobs rise each time it enables layoffs—as with Jack Dorsey’s Block. But whether AI replaces or empowers workers may depend on the choices made by companies and governments. Pro-worker AI may be possible.

title: Iran War: Trump’s secretary of state may have let the bloodcurdling truth slip
description: The Trump administration’s messaging of America's role in the Iran war is confusing at best and chilling at worst. The secretary of state's remark implying Trump followed Netanyahu’s lead exposes not only a lack of sovereign leadership, but inconsistencies in the White House view of US alliances

title: US-Israel’s Iran War blitz: Has China decided to hide its strength and bide its time?
description: China’s Gulf diplomacy could end up under the rubble of the Iran war even as its economy defies revival plans. Beijing has scaled back its GDP target. But what about its global power ambitions? There’s no sign of anything but a tactical pullback.

title: Mint Quick Edit | Russian oil: The US must accept that India will do what it must for its energy security
description: As the Iran war disrupts Gulf oil supply, India may be turning to Russian crude again, regardless of what Washington makes of it. It’s about time the US accepts that oil is an openly traded commodity—and that’s good for the global market.

title: Work, family, ambition, duty: Quit trying to balance priorities and reclaim your life by redefining success
description: The four decades of a career can become increasingly taxing, with burnout looming. Yet the real exhaustion is the erosion of the personal self between work and family. Instead of chasing a balance, redefine success.

title: Iran War: Presidential power has eclipsed the Congress' constitutional war powers. Here's why it matters
description: The US-Israel strike on Iran was an act of war. The Constitution requires Congress to authorize force, yet presidents have often bypassed it. Symbolic or not, passing a War Powers Resolution is one of the few ways Congress can reclaim its constitutional authority.

title: Are Indian states getting adequate fiscal support for the next five years?
description: The Finance Commission prescribed fewer grants this time, raising concern over whether states have enough support for the next half decade. However, states also receive non-statutory capital grants through the Centre’s special assistance scheme to fund essential public projects. Will this be enough?

title: Agentic advertising: Ad agencies will need to give up some of their creativity myths
description: For two years, the advertising industry debated whether AI can be creative. Meanwhile, audience engagement has shifted structurally. Agentic AI isn’t just a tool—it’s a leap that forces agencies to abandon creativity myths and focus on context, culture and other things machines struggle with.

title: India’s new GDP series is an upgrade but it leave a few questions on the economy unanswered
description: Recent changes in India’s GDP estimation promise a clearer view of the economy’s performance. Services dominate, but shifts in the shares of agriculture and manufacturing demand closer scrutiny for us to grasp trends and sharpen policy formulation.

title: Why the Iran war is not a setback for China, but a lesson—and maybe even an opportunity to exploit
description: Many see Trump’s Iran strikes as a setback for Beijing. But China’s strategy is to watch, wait and safeguard its core interests—oil flows, Taiwan and trade—while its geopolitical rival expends resources on war. If Washington gets bogged down in West Asia, could Xi turn crisis into opportunity?

title: Science is much more than just about discovery—it is a national sovereignty imperative today
description: Science is no longer just about advancing new technologies but is increasingly shaping how countries make economic choices across climate, AI and other frontiers. Countries that invest in foundational research will be better placed to shape standards in today’s fractured world

title: Andy Mukherjee: From oil and gold to capital flows and fertilizer, how the Iran conflict could rattle India’s economy
description: Past West Asian wars hit India at the petrol pump. This one could scorch far more—from remittances and smartphone exports to the rupee, fertilizer feedstock and the Centre’s gold-linked debt. How resilient is India’s economy to a prolonged Gulf inferno?

title: Not overly generous: Why a PF payout rate of 8.25% is worthy of celebration—and justified
description: The Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation has held its interest rate unchanged at 8.25% despite policy rate cuts by the Reserve Bank of India—and for good reason. It overlooks our short-term rate cycle and goes by returns made by EPFO.

title: Mint Quick Edit | War reverberations from West Asia: Why RBI may need to heighten its rupee vigil
description: India is armed with forex reserves but another gulf war has erupted at an unusually bad time for the Indian rupee. If war pressure intensifies, expect currency watchers to keep track of how the Reserve Bank manages the rupee’s managed float.
